description: User persona configuration for explicit communication style preferences.

columns.description: Primary column — display name of the user. One row per user (enforced by ownership).
User's preferred communication tone. Nullable — null means the agent infers tone from episodic memory patterns.
Preferred email sign-off (e.g., 'Best regards,', 'Thanks,', 'Cheers,'). Used by the Humanizer Agent for draft closing.
Preferred response formatting style. Nullable — null means the agent infers from episodic memory patterns.
Free-text rules for the agent (e.g., 'Never use exclamation marks', 'Always CC my assistant on procurement replies'). Injected into the agent system prompt at runtime.
Master toggle for persona preferences. When false, the agent ignores all persona settings and falls back to default behavior.
User's current graduated autonomy tier. Controls how much autonomous action the agent takes. Transitions are suggested by the system and confirmed by the user.
Cumulative count of card interactions (send, edit, dismiss). Used with cr_acceptancerate to determine autonomy tier eligibility. Incremented by Flow 5 (Card Outcome Tracker).
Ratio of (SENT_AS_IS + SENT_EDITED) / total outcomes. Used to gate autonomy tier transitions: ASSIST requires >0.70, PARTNER requires >0.85. Recomputed by Flow 5 on each outcome.

JSON blob with inferred tone preferences seeded from Graph bootstrap analysis of the user's own sent email patterns. Contains: formality_score, greeting_patterns[], signoff_patterns[], avg_sentence_length, emoji_usage_rate. Gives drafts a baseline before per-sender learning kicks in.

notes.design_rationale: One row per user. Explicit persona preferences take precedence over inferred semantic knowledge facts. When cr_preferredtone or cr_formattingstyle is null, the agent falls back to inferred facts from cr_semanticknowledge. Custom rules are injected verbatim into the system prompt — they are not validated.
notes.persona_promotion: When a user sets explicit preferences, corresponding semantic knowledge facts are created with cr_sourcetype = USER_EXPLICIT and cr_createdfrompersona = true. These facts have higher confidence and slower decay than inferred facts.
notes.security: Table uses UserOwned ownership type. Row-level security is enforced via an ownership-based security role so each user's persona is private.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically and do not need to be defined here.
